summary: The job failed when attempting to push a branch to the repository: git push returned HTTP 403 and the push command exited with code 128, causing the workflow to fail.
root_cause: git push to https://github.com/vercel/next.js/ failed with HTTP 403, indicating an authentication or permission failure when attempting to push the branch; the git push command exited with code 128.

evidence.quote: fatal: unable to access 'https://github.com/vercel/next.js/': The requested URL returned error: 403
evidence.reason: Shows the remote returned HTTP 403 when trying to access the repository, indicating an auth/permission problem.

evidence.quote: Error: Command failed with exit code 128 (Unknown system error -128): git push origin update/react/19.3.0-canary-142cfde8-20260422
evidence.reason: Shows the failing command was 'git push' for the branch and that it exited with code 128.
Additional metadata confirming the process exited with an error related to the git push failure.
Indicates the workflow failed as a result of the error.

suggested_fix: Verify and fix repository push authentication for the CI runner: ensure the workflow uses credentials with write permission (for example configure GITHUB_TOKEN or a deploy key with repo write access and enable repo permissions in the workflow), or adjust the git remote to use an authenticated URL. Then re-run the job.
